title: スライド作成skillはどうなった？
summary_text: **Findings (Lane A)** - Manus API calls are task-only. I found `POST /v1/tasks` and `GET /v1/tasks/{id}` only; no `/usage`, `/quota`, `/account`, `/profile` read/check endpoint usage in slide path code. [manus-api-client.js:64](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-api-client.js#L64) [manus-api-client.js:135](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-api-client.js#L135) [manus-research.js:489](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-research.js#L489) - API key/account selection is ambiguous if both keys exist: code always prefers `MANUS_API_KEY` over `MANUS_MCP_API_KEY`, with no explicit account fingerprint/logging. [manus-api-client.js:55](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-api-client.js#L55) [manus-research.js:474](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-research.js#L474) [manus-image-delegate.js:653](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-image-delegate.js#L653) - Profile is write-only (`currentProfile.profileKey`) and never prevalidated. Variant runner hardcodes `nano-banana`, so profile/account entitlement mismatch is possible and not disambiguated. [manus-slide-generator.js:707](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-slide-generator.js#L707) [manus-image-delegate.js:665](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-image-delegate.js#L665) [run-slide-variant-matrix.js:512](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-variant-matrix.js#L512) - Error semantics are lossy: HTTP/API errors are wrapped as `Network error: ...`, then regex-classified later. This can mislabel account/config issues as quota/network buckets. [manus-api-client.js:77](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-api-client.js#L77) [manus-api-client.js:101](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-api-client.js#L101) [run-slide-variant-matrix.js:120](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/run-slide-variant-matrix.js#L120) - `manus-research.js` does not gate on HTTP status before parsing body, so API failures can be structurally under-classified there. [manus-research.js:500](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/manus-research.js#L500) - Latest artifact evidence shows quota classification is regex-derived from message text, not from a dedicated quota endpoint response contract. [inspection-report.json:8](/Users/masayuki/Dev/tmp/note-medical-ai-gcloud-slides-20260318-v6-source-heavy/delivery-recheck/variants/nanobanana-manus/inspection-report.json#L8) - Test coverage exists for regex classification, but not for API-client-level account/quota/profile preflight behavior (single-lane failure interpretation remains). [test-unit-variant-matrix.js:83](/Users/masayuki/.codex/skills/slide/scripts/test-unit-variant-matrix.js#L83) **Likely live checks (minimal, high-signal)** - Run the same minimal `POST /v1/tasks` probe with each candidate key (`MANUS_API_KEY` only, then `MANUS_MCP_API_KEY` only), compare status/body. - Repeat probe with and without `currentProfile.profileKey: "nano-banana"` to isolate profile entitlement vs base quota. - Probe both hosts (`api.manus.ai`, `api.manus.im`) with same key/profile to detect host-tenant mismatch. - Log a key fingerprint prefix + selected env var source at runtime (no secret), so 429 can be tied to account source deterministically. - Keep variant-matrix classification, but add raw HTTP status/body capture from API client as first-class fields for postmortem.
